title: As pants the hart for cooling streams (revised)
firstLine: As pants the hart for cooling streams
publicDomainBasis: N. Tate (1652–1715), N. Brady (1639–1726), Hugh Wilson (1766–1824). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
As pants the hart for cooling streams
When heated in the chase,
So longs my soul, O God, for Thee,
And Thy refreshing grace.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.text:
O living God, my God, for Thee
My thirsty heart doth pine;
Oh, to draw nigh and thoroughly
Enjoy Thyself divine!

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.text:
My life, my strength, my dwelling place
Thou art, O God, to me;
In Christ Thou art enjoyed as grace
And fully gained by me.

sections.label: Verse 4
Verse 5
